The robotic arms are shown with several small red dots on top, which are magnets used for draping purposes. A clear plastic drape is placed over the arm, secured by snapping magnets into place on the magnet holes.

The narrator demonstrates the placement of the column drape on the robotic arm, highlighting the use of magnets to secure the drape. The column drape is opened and positioned on the column, with hands touching the outsides of the drape.
The presenter holds the outside of a square-shaped drape, aligning it with the magnet on the tower. They tear the tapes and pull the drape down, ensuring to handle only the outside of the drape.
The arm drape is placed over the robotic arm, starting at the base and moving upwards until reaching the magnets, which are then secured in place.

This is the arm drape as you're going to get it right out of the package on your field. So it's on your sterile field. Pop this tape open and fold it out. The drape is pulled up the robotic arm, and the magnet pad is attached. Excess slack is removed, and tape is undone to adjust the drape. The top and bottom plastic pieces are attached, ensuring no excess drape is underneath to prevent docking faults.

The surgeon adjusts the malleable plastic pieces on the drape, pressing them backwards to ensure the drape remains smooth and unobstructed for instrument insertion into the trocars.
